title: REPLICATION AUDIT
INTERPRETATION: Heavy tails fully explained by all controls. Low-dimensional structure survives burst timing control, consistent with Phase 179 finding. Channel perm is expected to survive (PCA is invariant).
LIMITATIONS: IAAFT omitted due to timeout risk (Phase 179 showed 600s+)
Simple holdout instead of full LOSO (for speed)
Burst timing control shows surviving structure - suggests low-dim arises from burst patterns themselves

assumptions: 90th percentile burst threshold identical to Phase 177
4 controls: A, C, D, E (F omitted)
runtime_substitutions: IAAFT (F) -> OMITTED due to 600s+ timeout in Phase 179
INTEGRITY_REPORT.leakage_checks: holdout instead of LOSO - potential concern but acceptable for replication audit
INTEGRITY_REPORT.confidence: HIGH for Q3, MODERATE for Q4

key_insight: Burst timing shuffle DOES NOT destroy low-dimensional coordination - confirms Phase 179 finding that low-dim structure arises from burst timing patterns, not unique neural mechanism
